behavior_summary: Implements Vividos' ritual/cutscene (the 'Ceremony of Eternity') that runs when the instance's U8P_VIVIDOS::cast event is triggered and its target is the Avatar who is dead.
Primary guard: the caster's target must equal the Avatar (target == 1) and that target must be dead — only then does the main ritual proceed.

When triggered the script places the Avatar into stasis, sequences dialog lines (Item::bark), schedules timed/visual effects via spawned helper threads (METHOD::15E7, FREE::*), starts a camera quake and SFX, performs an HP/dir/frame-based effect call (METHOD::0080), spawns/places multiple NPCs and directs them to pathfind beside an altar, and later stops the quake and returns.
Timing and pacing are implemented by spawning many small helper threads and synchronizing with spawn/imply/suspend patterns rather than by blocking waits.
notable_control_flow: Entry is an Event 11 'U8P_VIVIDOS::cast' handler (U8P_VIVIDOS::VIVIDOS::cast). It first reads Npc::getTarget and only continues if result == 1 and Npc::isDead(target) is true (guard).

The main sequence sets avatar stasis (setAvatarInStasis(1)), then spawns multiple METHOD::15E7 and FREE::* helper threads used as timers; the script consumes their returns with imply/pop patterns to synchronize pacing.

A long helper (U8P_VIVIDOS::VIVIDOS::01D7) encapsulates the full ceremony: dialog, camera quakes, SFX, coordinate lookups (Npc::getMap, Item::getZ/Y/X), area visual spawns, NPC creation/pop/orStatus, and pathfind commands.
The script relies heavily on asynchronous spawned helpers for sequencing; control returns only after quakes are stopped and spawned sequences have completed or been synchronized.
open_questions: Exact semantics and timing behavior of METHOD::15E7 and the various FREE::* spawned helpers — they appear to be timer/delay/effect drivers but their internal behavior is not shown.
Whether target ID == 1 always maps to the Avatar in all contexts (it is inferred but treated as the Avatar here).
Precise meanings of status flags set via Item::orStatus on created NPCs (which bits are applied and their gameplay effects).
How many of the spawned NPCs remain persistent versus being purely cinematic helpers (the script creates multiple NPCs but their lifecycle beyond the scene is unclear).

main_function.name: U8P_VIVIDOS::cast
main_function.purpose: Gate and execute Vividos' ritual/cutscene when the instance 'casts' — verify the target is the Avatar and dead, freeze the Avatar, run a timed sequence of dialog, effects, camera quakes, SFX and spawn/move NPCs to stage the ceremony, then restore/cleanup.
entry_conditions: Invoked as Event 11 (U8P_VIVIDOS::cast) for the Vividos instance.
Npc::getTarget() must return 1 (inferred to be the Avatar).
Npc::isDead(target) must be true (the Avatar is dead).

function_map.name: U8P_VIVIDOS::cast
function_map.role: Event 11 handler that gates and initiates the ceremony/ritual sequence.

function_map.name: U8P_VIVIDOS::01D7
function_map.role: Implements the extended 'Ceremony of Eternity' cutscene: dialog, camera control, SFX, NPC creation/placement and choreography.

function_map.name: Npc::getTarget
function_map.role: Return caster's current target id (used for the main guard).

function_map.name: Npc::isDead
function_map.role: Test whether the target/actor is dead (guard gating the ritual).

function_map.name: U8P_VIVIDOS::setAvatarInStasis
function_map.role: Freeze/unfreeze the Avatar to prevent player input during the cinematic.

function_map.name: METHOD::15E7
function_map.role: Spawned repeatedly as a timing/effect step driver to pace the sequence (inferred).

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Spawned helper thread used as a short wait/timer or small effect helper (inferred).

function_map.name: FREE::1C6D
function_map.role: Spawned to create an area visual/effect at map coordinates (inferred).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Emit dialog lines from Vividos and other participants during the ceremony.

function_map.name: Camera::startQuake
function_map.role: Begin a screen/world quake effect for dramatic emphasis.

function_map.name: U8P_VIVIDOS::playSFX
function_map.role: Play sound effects to accompany ceremony moments.

function_map.name: Camera::stopQuake
function_map.role: Stop the quake effect started earlier.

function_map.name: Npc::create
function_map.role: Create NPC instances (ambassadors/undead) used in the ceremony.

function_map.name: Item::orStatus
function_map.role: Set status flags on created NPCs (e.g., hide/invulnerable) for staging.

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: Place spawned NPCs at coordinates on the map.

function_map.name: Npc::pathfind
function_map.role: Command created NPCs to move to staging positions beside the altar.

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Apply an animation/effect using HP/dir/frame parameters (inferred).

internal_calls.purpose: Invoke the long scripted ceremony/cutscene sequence after guard checks and initial pacing.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the caster's current target id to check whether it is the Avatar (==1).
Check that the target (inferred Avatar) is dead before proceeding with the ritual.
Freeze the Avatar (stasis on) for the duration of the ceremony.
Spawned repeatedly to schedule/deliver timed effect or pacing steps during the ritual (inferred as a timer/effect driver).
Spawn a short helper thread used as a wait or small effect synchronizer.
Deliver multiple dialog lines spoken by Vividos and other cinematic participants during the ceremony.
Begin a quake effect during key ritual moments.
Play sound effects synchronized with quakes/dialog for dramatic effect.
Apply a visual/animation effect using NPC HP, direction and frame inputs (inferred from pushed args).
Get map id to spawn area visuals at correct map context.
Obtain Z (height) coordinate for area visual placement and NPC pop positions.
Obtain Y coordinate for area visual placement and NPC pop positions.
Obtain X coordinate for area visual placement and NPC pop positions.
Spawn an area visual/effect at the obtained coordinates (inferred).
Create NPC instances (ambassadors/undead) used to populate the ceremony scene.
Set staging status bits on newly created NPCs (e.g., hide/invulnerable/immobile flags) so they behave as cinematic actors.
Place the created NPCs at computed coordinates near the altar.
Order created NPCs to walk to specified positions beside the altar to stage the ritual choreography.
Poll an item's frame/state in a loop to trigger subsequent choreography when a frame target is reached (inferred).
Stop the camera/world quake at the end of the cinematic sequence.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements Vividos' ritual/cutscene (the 'Ceremony of Eternity') that runs when the instance's U8P_VIVIDOS::cast event is triggered and its target is the Avatar who is dead.
